expectations.text: ProductCard 구현이 하나의 source of truth로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: Only one file exists in outputs: components/ProductCard/index.tsx. The flat components/ProductCard.tsx was deleted. Transcript confirms: '단일 source of truth: components/ProductCard/index.tsx'.
No file at components/ProductCard.tsx in outputs directory. Only components/ProductCard/index.tsx exists. Transcript states 'DELETED: components/ProductCard.tsx'.
Output file is located at components/ProductCard/index.tsx, matching the folder/index pattern.

With a single source at components/ProductCard/index.tsx, all imports resolve to '@/components/ProductCard' or '../components/ProductCard'. However, no actual import usages exist in the outputs to verify consumer-side consistency — this passes based on the structural guarantee of a single source.

expectations.text: hover, image, price 등 visual affordance가 보존된다
expectations.evidence: Hover: 'group-hover:scale-105' (line 35) and 'hover:shadow-md' (line 27). Image: Next.js Image component with aspect-square container (line 30-37). Price: '{currency}{price.toLocaleString("ko-KR")}' (lines 51-52).

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: The full contents of index.tsx contain no useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation calls. Only imports are 'use client' directive and 'next/image'.
Folder name is 'ProductCard' (PascalCase), not 'productCard' (camelCase). Output path: components/ProductCard/index.tsx.
File is at components/ProductCard/index.tsx, not components/common/productCard/index.tsx. There is no 'common' directory in the output structure.

claims.claim: components/ProductCard.tsx (flat) had fewer features than ProductCard/index.tsx
claims.evidence: Transcript lists flat version props as (imageUrl, name, price, currency) vs folder version with additional (imageAlt, badge, testId) plus hover scale, shadow, badge, and line-clamp-2. The surviving file confirms these features.

claims.claim: folder/index pattern is the project convention based on Button and DashboardLayout
claims.evidence: Transcript documents checking components/Button/index.tsx and components/DashboardLayout/ as evidence of convention. This is a reasonable convention detection approach.

claims.claim: All visual affordances from the flat version are preserved in the kept version
claims.evidence: Flat version had rounded corners (rounded-xl -> rounded-2xl), image display (h-48 -> aspect-square), truncation (truncate -> line-clamp-2), and price display. All preserved or improved in the surviving file.

suggestions.reason: No consumer files exist in the outputs, so import consistency cannot actually be verified from the output artifacts. This assertion passes on structural inference only. Including at least one file that imports ProductCard would make this assertion verifiable.

This assertion conflicts with the actual project convention shown in the transcript (Button/, DashboardLayout/ are PascalCase). The eval asserts camelCase but the project itself uses PascalCase folders. Consider whether this assertion reflects the actual desired convention or is incorrect.

The transcript shows existing components live directly under components/ (Button/, DashboardLayout/), not under components/common/. This assertion may be testing an aspirational structure that differs from the actual project layout, making it likely to fail when the skill correctly follows existing conventions.

eval_feedback.overall: Two failed assertions (camelCase folder, common/ placement) appear to conflict with the actual project conventions the skill correctly detected. The skill followed existing patterns (PascalCase, components/ root) rather than the eval's expectations. Consider aligning assertions with the project's actual conventions, or clarifying that the skill should override existing conventions.
